description: 20 inbound guest-email test cases with gold-standard answers/actions for the Grand Oslo Hotel email agent. Each case exercises the agent end-to-end (parse -> classify -> plan -> approval_gate -> execute) and asserts on PMS reads (behavior) and PMS writes (mutation). Gold standards were computed programmatically against src/hotel_agent (pms.py, workflows.py, guardrails.py), not by hand.
assumptions: Treat the mock PMS calendar (April 2025) as the active booking window; ignore the wall-clock date. The planner is given 'today' at runtime but availability only exists for the listed April 2025 dates.
Each case runs against a FRESH copy of data/mock_hotel_data.json (cases are independent). That is why every new-booking case creates reservation RES007 and every new guest becomes G006.
check_out is exclusive: a stay 2025-04-20 -> 2025-04-22 occupies the nights of the 20th and 21st (2 nights).
created_at on newly written records equals the run date (date.today()), so tests should not assert its exact value.

matching_notes.expected.read_tool_calls: The minimal read-only tools the planner SHOULD call. Order-independent; extra read calls (e.g. list_inventory to map names to ids) are acceptable and should not fail a case.
matching_notes.expected.plan_actions: Gold-standard write workflow(s) the plan should contain. An empty list means the correct behavior is read-only (no PMS mutation).
matching_notes.expected.pms_mutation: The net effect on the PMS after execute. 'writes:false' means the PMS state must be byte-for-byte unchanged.
matching_notes.expected.reply_must_include: Case-insensitive substrings/facts the draft guest reply should contain.
matching_notes.expected.reply_must_not: Behaviors the reply must NOT exhibit (e.g. confirming a refund on a non-refundable booking).
matching_notes.approval: auto_approved = ran end-to-end in auto mode; human_required = pauses in human mode only; blocked_pending_human = pauses for a human in BOTH modes (guardrail).
matching_notes.scoring_hint: A case passes when parsed.intent, risk.risky, plan_actions (workflow+key args), pms_mutation, approval and final_status all match, and the reply satisfies must_include / must_not.

scenario_legend.1: Read-only lookup: plan uses read tools, no PMS write.
scenario_legend.2: Action + write: full plan -> approve -> execute (a mutation happens).
scenario_legend.3: Ambiguous / risky: flagged by guardrails, never auto-executed, routed to human in BOTH modes.

cases.title: Simple availability lookup
cases.email: Hi, do you have any rooms available from 20 to 22 April? Thanks.

reply_must_not: invents a room type not in inventory

cases.asserts: All four room types are free for 20-21 Apr; agent lists them and writes nothing.

cases.title: Rate quote for a specific room + rate plan
cases.email: What would a Superior Double with breakfast cost for two adults from 24 to 26 April?
cases.asserts: Gold quote = 5000 room + 1000 breakfast = 6000 NOK for RT003 + RP002 over 2 nights; no write.

cases.title: Cancellation policy question
cases.email: What is your cancellation policy?
cases.asserts: Answers from get_policy('cancellation'); no PMS write.

cases.title: Pet policy question
cases.email: Can I bring my small dog? He's about 8 kg.
cases.asserts: 8 kg is under the 10 kg limit; agent confirms pet-friendly rooms at 300 NOK/night; no write.

cases.title: Booking-details lookup by email
cases.email: Could you remind me what I've got booked with you? — Erik Hansen, erik.hansen@email.com
cases.asserts: Erik (G001) has two active reservations RES001 (Standard Double, 20-23 Apr) and RES004 (Superior Double, 10-12 May); agent reads and lists them, writes nothing.
cases.known_gap: The current INTENTS taxonomy has no 'reservation_lookup' value, so this maps to intent 'other'. In guardrails.py, 'other' is in _AMBIGUOUS_INTENTS, so the deterministic guard would flag it 'ambiguous' and route it to human review (risky=true). Gold standard above is the DESIRED behavior. Recommendation for the reviewer: add a read-only 'reservation_lookup' intent so pure lookups are answered autonomously instead of being escalated.

cases.title: Availability filtered by party size
cases.email: We're a family of three (2 adults, 1 child). Which rooms fit us for the night of 24 April?
cases.asserts: Party of 3 excludes RT001 (max 1) and RT002 (max 2); only RT003 and RT004 are returned; no write.

cases.title: Near-sold-out date edge case
cases.email: Any availability for the night of 26 April?
cases.asserts: On 26 Apr only RT004 (Junior Suite) has stock; the other three are at 0; agent offers only the Junior Suite.

cases.title: New booking for an existing guest (standard rate)
cases.email: Hi, this is Erik Hansen (erik.hansen@email.com). I'd like to book a Standard Double for 2 adults from 18 to 20 April on your standard rate.

reply_must_not: creates a duplicate guest for Erik
charges breakfast on a room-only rate

cases.asserts: Reuses guest G001 (no new guest), creates RES007 at 3600 NOK (1800 x 2 nights), decrements RT002 stock on the 18th and 19th.
cases.human_mode_behavior: In --mode human the run pauses at the approval gate (awaiting_approval); on --approve the same mutation is committed; on --reject nothing is written.

cases.title: New booking for a NEW guest, breakfast included
cases.email: I'd like to reserve a Junior Suite with breakfast for 2 adults, 24-26 April. Name: Sofia Rossi, email sofia.rossi@email.com, phone +39 02 1234567, Italy.
cases.asserts: Creates new guest G006, then RES007 at 8600 NOK (7600 room + 1000 breakfast), zeroing RT004 stock on the 24th and 25th.
cases.human_mode_behavior: In --mode human the run pauses at the gate; approve -> same mutation, reject -> no write and no new guest.

cases.title: New booking on the Flexible rate (existing guest)
cases.email: James Smith here (james.smith@email.com). Please book me a Standard Double on the Flexible Rate for the night of 18 April (out on the 19th), 2 adults.

reply_must_not: creates a duplicate guest for James
applies the standard rate instead of flexible

cases.asserts: Reuses guest G003, creates RES007 at 2070 NOK (1800 x 1.15 modifier x 1 night), decrements RT002 on the 18th.
cases.human_mode_behavior: In --mode human, pauses at the gate; approve -> same mutation, reject -> no write.

cases.title: Modify an existing reservation's dates (refundable)
cases.email: Could you move my reservation RES001 to check in 18 April and check out 21 April instead? — Erik
cases.asserts: RES001 is on RP001 (standard, refundable) so change is allowed; dates move to 18-21 Apr, total stays 5400 NOK (3 nights). Old nights (20/21/22) are released and new nights (18/19/20) are taken; net availability delta is as shown (the 20th cancels out).
cases.human_mode_behavior: In --mode human, pauses at the gate; approve -> same mutation, reject -> reservation unchanged.

cases.title: Modify a reservation by changing rate plan (add breakfast)
cases.email: For booking RES001, can we switch to the breakfast-included rate? — Erik Hansen
cases.asserts: Switching RES001 to RP002 keeps dates/room but recomputes total to 6900 NOK (5400 room + 1500 breakfast for 2 guests x 3 nights). No availability change.
cases.human_mode_behavior: In --mode human, pauses at the gate; approve -> same mutation, reject -> rate plan unchanged.

cases.title: Cancel a refundable (flexible) reservation
cases.email: Please cancel my reservation RES003.

reply_must_not: refuses to cancel a flexible booking

cases.asserts: RES003 is on RP004 (flexible) so cancellation is allowed and not risky; status flips confirmed->cancelled and RT004 stock is released on the 25th and 26th.
cases.human_mode_behavior: In --mode human, pauses at the gate; approve -> same cancellation, reject -> reservation stays confirmed.
cases.note: The flexible policy implies a possible 50% charge within 7 days, which the current guardrail does not flag. Possible enhancement: surface flexible-window charges for confirmation.

cases.title: Booking request for an unavailable room type
cases.email: I'd like to book a Standard Double for 2 adults on 22 April (out on the 23rd). — Anna Berg, anna.berg@email.com

reply_must_not: invents availability that does not exist

cases.asserts: RT002 is sold out on 22 Apr (0), so the correct behavior is NO write: the agent reports the Standard Double is unavailable and offers the alternatives that fit 2 adults (RT003 Superior Double, RT004 Junior Suite).
cases.acceptable_degraded: If the planner still proposes make_reservation(RT002), the write MUST fail safely: create_reservation raises PMSError 'No Standard Double available for 2025-04-22 to 2025-04-23.', workflow ok=false, PMS unchanged (writes:false), final_status 'completed_with_errors'. It must never fabricate a confirmation.

cases.title: Refund request on a non-refundable booking
cases.email: I need a refund on my booking RES002, we can't make it. — Maria Gonzalez (maria.gonzalez@email.com)
cases.asserts: RES002 is on RP003 (non-refundable). Two guardrails fire: financial_request ('refund' keyword) + non_refundable_change (data signal). In AUTO mode the run still pauses for a human, plan.actions is stripped to [], and RES002 stays confirmed.

cases.title: Cancel a non-refundable booking
cases.email: Please cancel my non-refundable booking RES005. — Yuki Tanaka
cases.asserts: RES005 is on RP003 (non-refundable). non_refundable_change fires from both the data signal and the word 'non-refundable'; financial_request ALSO fires because 'refund' is a substring of 'non-refundable'. Blocked in auto mode; RES005 stays confirmed.
cases.note: The financial_request match here is a substring artifact ('refund' inside 'non-refundable'). It does not change the outcome (still correctly blocked) but is worth noting for the reviewer.

cases.title: Compensation demand (no reservation id)
cases.email: The construction noise ruined our stay and I expect compensation for the inconvenience. — james.smith@email.com
cases.asserts: 'compensation' triggers financial_request; risky in auto mode. Agent empathizes and forwards to a human without promising money or admitting fault. No PMS write.

cases.title: Request to waive a fee (financial, but refundable rate)
cases.email: Can you waive the cancellation fee on RES001? I know it's short notice.
cases.asserts: RES001 is on RP001 (refundable), so non_refundable_change does NOT fire, but 'waive' triggers financial_request independently. Demonstrates the financial-keyword guard blocks money decisions regardless of rate plan. No PMS write.

cases.title: Vague / ambiguous message
cases.email: Hi, I have a question about my upcoming stay.
cases.asserts: Intent is 'other' -> ambiguous flag -> routed to human in both modes. Agent must not guess or take a PMS action; a clarifying/holding reply is expected.

cases.title: Threat to dispute a card charge (chargeback)
cases.email: I'm going to dispute the charge on my card for booking RES003 if this isn't sorted. — James
cases.asserts: 'dispute' + 'charge' trigger financial_request; risky in auto mode. Agent de-escalates and forwards to a human; RES003 (flexible) is untouched despite being technically refundable — money/dispute language always goes to a person. No PMS write.
